expectations.text: Report identifies the token expiry contradiction (1 hour vs 24 hours)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #1 in table: 'Token expiry duration — routes.md: expires_in: 3600 (1 hour) — sdk-docs.md: Token is valid for 24 hours'

expectations.text: Report identifies the account deletion behaviour contradiction (soft-delete vs permanent)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #2: 'Account deletion behaviour — routes.md: Soft-deletes the user account + 30-day recovery window — sdk-docs.md: permanent and immediate, cannot be undone'

expectations.text: Report identifies the login rate limit contradiction (5 vs 10 attempts per minute)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #4: 'Login rate limit — routes.md: 5 attempts per minute — sdk-docs.md: max 10 per minute'

expectations.text: Report identifies the project limit contradiction (10 free-tier vs unlimited)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #6: 'Free-tier project limit — routes.md: Max 10 projects per free-tier user — sdk-docs.md: Free tier: unlimited projects'

expectations.text: Report uses the changelog to inform which contradictions have a clear resolution
expectations.evidence: Section 'Temporal analysis (using changelog as timeline)' resolves #3, #5, pagination, and colour via changelog. Section 'Resolved via changelog (temporal authority)' provides explicit fixes. Section 'Needs your call' separates unresolved items.

expectations.text: Report identifies the colour/color format contradiction (hex-only vs any CSS value)
expectations.evidence: Under 'Temporal analysis': 'Colour field: Changelog says changed to hex-only in v2.3.0. Routes.md says valid hex code — current. SDK-docs says accepts any CSS color value — stale since v2.3.0.' Also in 'Terminology Drift' section.

expectations.text: Each finding includes specific file references and quoted values
expectations.evidence: Every contradiction in the table cites the source file (routes.md, sdk-docs.md, changelog.md) and quotes the conflicting values (e.g., 'expires_in: 3600', 'Token is valid for 24 hours', '5 attempts per minute', 'max 10 per minute').

expectations.text: After the report, the skill offers to resolve contradictions interactively
expectations.evidence: Final line of report: 'Want me to update the files to resolve these? (Pending your answers to the preference questions above.)' — interactive resolution offer with pending questions listed.

claims.evidence: The table lists 6 numbered contradictions, each citing specific file pairs

claims.claim: Changelog resolves several contradictions with temporal authority
claims.evidence: The report correctly identifies that changelog v2.3.0 entries provide a timeline that resolves #3 (recovery window), #5 (email verification), pagination, and colour format

claims.claim: All contradictions are classified as 'preference' type
claims.evidence: Classification table shows all 6 as 'Preference' — correct since these are internal project decisions with no external standard

suggestions.assertion: Report identifies the account deletion behaviour contradiction (soft-delete vs permanent)
suggestions.reason: The report actually splits this into two separate contradictions (#2 soft vs permanent, #3 recovery window duration). The assertion passes either way, but a tighter assertion could check that the report distinguishes these as separate issues.

No assertion checks whether the report correctly identifies the email verification contradiction (routes.md says required, changelog removed it). The skill found this as contradiction #5 — a valuable catch that goes untested.

No assertion checks the quality of the resolution suggestions (e.g., whether proposed fixes are actionable and correct). The report provides specific fix instructions for changelog-resolved items — worth verifying.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong eval set. All 8 assertions are discriminating — they require genuine analysis, not surface compliance. The two gaps noted above (email verification detection and fix quality) are minor since the core contradictions are well covered.
